content_text:
The midday sun was relentless, and the forecast warned of record-breaking temperatures. You were assigned to set up equipment on the outdoor staging area, a task that usually took an hour but felt like an eternity under the scorching heat. As the first sweat dripped into your eyes, you noticed your coworker glancing at the clock repeatedly, clearly eager to finish before the peak heat of the afternoon.

You paused for a moment to take a Rate Your State (RYS) check, assessing how the heat was affecting your focus and energy. Realizing you were starting to feel the effects, you suggested a short break and hydration pause. You also ran through Anticipating Error, thinking about the risks of fatigue and reduced awareness during the rest of the task. By planning ahead and addressing the heat's impact early, you helped keep the team safe and focused through the rest of the shift.
